summary: Credential and wallet harvester masquerading as a secrets-detection tool
details: Package name advertises a Web3 secrets detector but ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) whose code structure matches a credential and crypto-wallet stealer. scanner.js contains a hardcoded exfiltration endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 161 and 323) reached via https.request/https.get POSTs, alongside execSync calls that gather host identity (os.hostname(), whoami) for transmission. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem enumeration paired with multiple POST endpoints and curl invocations, consistent with browser/wallet-data harvesting and outbound exfiltration. build-verifier.js combines child_process, https, fs.existsSync probing and an execSync('docker push...') call, indicating credential staging and pushing artifacts to attacker-controlled infrastructure. The combination of (a) a hardcoded C2 on a free GitHub Pages-style host, (b) wallet-scanner enumeration of the filesystem, and (c) host-info collection routed to outbound HTTPS POSTs is a credential/wallet-stealer fingerprint. Installing or loading this package exposes installer environment data, host identity, and any wallet/credential artifacts on disk to the operator of ddjidd564.github.io.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
